name: attestplane
description: Verifiable audit substrate for AI agents — EU AI Act Article 12-aligned. Apache 2.0.
description_good_justification: README provides a one-paragraph project description at the top: 'Verifiable audit substrate for AI agents — EU AI Act Article 12-aligned'.
interact_justification: GitHub Issues and Discussions enabled; CONTRIBUTING.md documents the PR + DCO sign-off contribution flow; SECURITY.md documents the private vulnerability reporting channel.
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md at https://github.com/attestplane/attestplane/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md covers DCO sign-off (§1), branch and commit conventions (§2-3), PR checklist (§4), review expectations, and the red→green→refactor TDD rhythm.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md §4 PR Checklist at https://github.com/attestplane/attestplane/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md enumerates required test coverage, DCO sign-off, CHANGELOG entry, and lint/type-check gates as acceptance requirements.
floss_license_justification: Released under Apache-2.0; SPDX-License-Identifier declared via REUSE 3.3; LICENSES/ directory present.
license_location_justification: LICENSE file at https://github.com/attestplane/attestplane/blob/main/LICENSE (repository root); NOTICE file and LICENSES/ directory present per REUSE 3.3 specification.
documentation_basics_justification: README plus docs/ tree covering adr/, architecture/, security/, runbooks/, spec/, release/, and validation/.
documentation_interface_justification: Python SDK README (sdk/python/README.md) and TypeScript SDK README (sdk/typescript/README.md) document the public API surface; canonical wire format in docs/spec/.
sites_https_justification: Project homepage attestplane.io, repository github.com, and distribution channels pypi.org and npmjs.com are all HTTPS-only.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues and Discussions enabled; both are searchable, URL-addressable, and require no proprietary client-side software.
english_justification: Primary docs (README, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, GOVERNANCE, CHANGELOG, ADRs) are all in English; CONTRIBUTING_zh.md is an additional Chinese translation, not a divergent governance document.
maintained_justification: Active development with multiple commits per day; v1.0 GA milestone (full SLA) targeted for 2026-08-15 per SECURITY.md; pre-GA tag line currently at v1.0.3 (2026-05-21).
repo_track_justification: Full git history preserved; every commit carries author identity (GitHub privacy email) and timestamp; DCO sign-off line required on PRs per CONTRIBUTING.md §1 (enforced on inbound contributions).
repo_interim_justification: Atomic commits using Conventional Commits format; recent history shows feature/fix/chore/docs separation between releases (e.g. PR #22, #23, #8).
version_unique_justification: Each release tag is unique (v0.0.1-alpha through the v1.0.x line); tag scheme documented in CHANGELOG.md preamble.
version_semver_justification: CHANGELOG.md preamble declares adherence to Semantic Versioning 2.0; pre-release suffixes (-alpha, -beta.N, -rc.N) follow SemVer §9–11.
version_tags_justification: Every release has a corresponding vX.Y.Z annotated git tag; release-cd workflow validates tag immutability per ADR-0017.
release_notes_justification: CHANGELOG.md at https://github.com/attestplane/attestplane/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog 1.1; per-release sections summarise notable changes; GitHub Releases publish per-tag notes.
release_notes_vulns_justification: Pre-GA line has no published CVEs to date; SECURITY.md commits to crediting reporters and identifying CVE-assigned vulnerabilities in release notes when fixed.
report_process_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md document two channels: public GitHub Issues for general bug reports, SECURITY.md private process for vulnerabilities.
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues used as the canonical bug tracker.
report_responses_justification: Recent issues triaged within days (e.g. dependabot PR #8 reviewed and merged same window; issue #7 design questions answered with documented closure). Pre-GA cadence; formal SLA published in SECURITY.md applies from v1.0 GA onwards.
enhancement_responses_justification: Feature requests routed through Issues and Discussions; v0.8 → v0.9 line shipped community-requested enhancements (e.g. issue #18 Sigstore signing closed by PR #22).
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues at https://github.com/attestplane/attestplane/issues retains full history; closed issues remain publicly readable and searchable by URL.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md at https://github.com/attestplane/attestplane/blob/main/SECURITY.md 'Reporting a Vulnerability' section documents the private email channel and GitHub Security Advisories path; public issues for vulnerabilities are prohibited.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: Private channels are documented in SECURITY.md (email and GitHub Security Advisories), both encrypted in transit. GPG key publication is tracked for v1.0 GA.
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md 'Response Timeline' section commits to acknowledgement within 7 days; the published SLA applies from v1.0 GA (target 2026-08-15) onwards. Pre-GA: best-effort triage on the same cadence.
build_justification: Documented build paths: 'pip install -e sdk/python[dev]' and 'npm install' in sdk/typescript; release-cd workflow exercises the full build path.
build_common_tools_justification: Standard ecosystem build tools: hatchling (PEP 517) and uv for Python; npm for TypeScript.
build_floss_tools_justification: All build toolchain (hatchling, uv, npm) is OSI-approved FLOSS.
test_justification: pytest tests under sdk/python/tests/ and tests/; vitest tests under sdk/typescript/test/; cross-SDK byte-equivalence tests via scripts/test-cross-sdk-roundtrip.sh and the cross-sdk-roundtrip GitHub Actions workflow.
test_invocation_justification: Standard invocation documented in CONTRIBUTING.md §2: 'pytest sdk/python/' for Python and 'cd sdk/typescript && npm install && npm test' for TypeScript; PR Checklist §4 enforces both pass.
test_most_justification: Per-PR CI runs pytest + vitest + cross-SDK conformance + invariants gates; CONTRIBUTING.md PR checklist requires test coverage for new public APIs and state transitions.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: ci.yml workflow triggers on every push to main and every pull_request; concurrency-grouped per ref; supplemented by daily CodeQL, OSV-Scanner, and weekly OSSF Scorecard.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md §3 documents the red→green→refactor TDD rhythm and requires a failing test first for new behaviour.
tests_are_added_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md §4 PR checklist requires new public APIs, state transitions, and framework mappings to ship with test coverage; recent PRs (e.g. #22, #23) included the corresponding test changes.
tests_documented_added_justification: ADR template (docs/adr/0000-template.md) includes a test-coverage section; CONTRIBUTING.md §3-4 documents the testing policy and PR checklist obligations.
warnings_justification: ruff and mypy --strict for Python; biome and tsc --noEmit for TypeScript; codespell, typos, yamllint, markdownlint, actionlint, shellcheck, REUSE compliance all run in CI.
warnings_fixed_justification: ruff, mypy, biome, and tsc all fail-closed in CI; PR cannot merge while warnings outstanding (recent example: PR #23 fixed RUF043 lint regression).
warnings_strict_justification: mypy --strict mode; ruff with project rule set including RUF/E/F families; biome strict; all elevated to CI errors.
know_secure_design_justification: SECURITY.md documents the threat model and substrate-level secure design choices; GOVERNANCE.md captures the maintainer's security responsibilities. Pre-GA: no third-party security audit.
know_common_errors_justification: SECURITY.md threat categories enumerate project-specific failure modes (chain tampering, replay, anchor forgery, supply-chain compromise, claim drift); hardening guidance in the same document.
crypto_published_justification: All cryptographic primitives are public standards: SHA-256 (FIPS 180-4), Ed25519 (RFC 8032), RFC 3161 TSA, Sigstore/cosign. No proprietary algorithms.
crypto_call_justification: Python pyca/cryptography and Node @noble/ed25519 / WebCrypto used for SHA-256 and Ed25519; no in-house primitive implementations.
crypto_floss_justification: pyca/cryptography is Apache-2.0/BSD; @noble/ed25519 is MIT; both OSI-approved FLOSS.
crypto_keylength_justification: SHA-256 (256-bit output) and Ed25519 (~128-bit equivalent security) meet 2026 NIST SP 800-57 guidance for the project's threat model; RFC 3161 anchor chains validated against TSA certificate constraints per ADR-0003 and ADR-0005.
crypto_working_justification: SHA-256, Ed25519, and RFC 3161 are non-broken as of 2026-05-21 per NIST and ENISA published guidance. Pre-GA: no third-party cryptanalysis audit of project-specific protocol composition.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: No known-weak primitives (MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, etc.) used anywhere in the codebase; primitive selection documented in ADR-0005 event signing scheme.
crypto_pfs_justification: Project provides evidence-substrate primitives, not a TLS-terminating network service; forward secrecy is the transport layer's responsibility (operator-deployed TLS to TSA endpoints).
crypto_password_storage_justification: Project does not store user passwords; authentication is delegated to operator-deployed gateways per SECURITY.md hardening guidance.
crypto_random_justification: OS CSPRNG via pyca/cryptography (os.urandom / secrets) and Node crypto.randomBytes / webcrypto.getRandomValues; no insecure RNG in security-sensitive paths.
delivery_mitm_justification: Releases distributed via PyPI, npm, and GitHub Releases — all HTTPS-only. Forward-only Sigstore keyless cosign signatures and SLSA Build L3 provenance attached to new releases per ADR-0018.
delivery_unsigned_justification: Forward-only Sigstore keyless cosign + SLSA Build L3 provenance per ADR-0018 (workflows sign-release.yml and slsa-provenance.yml). Verification recipe in docs/release/verifying-signatures.md. Pre-ADR-0018 tags are not retroactively signed.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: SECURITY.md Response Timeline commits to Critical ≤30 days, High ≤60 days, Medium/Low ≤90 days from v1.0 GA onwards. No unfixed public CVEs against the pre-GA line.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No known unfixed Critical vulnerabilities as of 2026-05-21; OSV-Scanner runs daily and CodeQL on every push.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: OSV-Scanner CI workflow runs daily; gitleaks invariants enforced in invariants.yml; no committed credentials. GitHub secret-scanning push-protection enabled at org level.
static_analysis_justification: CodeQL workflow on every push and pull_request plus weekly schedule; ruff and mypy --strict enforced in CI on every commit.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: CodeQL default query packs cover CWE Top 25 patterns; OSV-Scanner runs daily for dependency CVEs; OSSF Scorecard runs weekly.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: Static-analysis findings either fixed before merge or tracked as GitHub Security Advisories; CI fails closed on new High or Critical findings.
static_analysis_often_justification: CodeQL on every push and PR plus weekly cron; OSV-Scanner daily; OSSF Scorecard weekly; ruff/mypy on every CI run.
dynamic_analysis_justification: Hypothesis property-based tests in sdk/python/tests/; nightly mutation testing via mutation.yml; deterministic fault-injection harness in scripts/check-fault-injection.sh.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: Load-bearing code is Python and TypeScript (both memory-safe); no unsafe memory operations in scope.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Python assert enabled in tests; TypeScript runtime invariants via vitest assertions; ProofBundle verifier predicates fail-closed on assertion violation.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Mutation-test gaps tracked in advisory issues; hypothesis test failures block CI; no deferred Medium-plus findings outstanding.
